body: Hi Priya, Dr. Meera's Dental Clinic here. It's been a while since your last visit, and your 6-month cleaning recall is due. Apke liye 2 slots ready hain: Wed 5 Nov, 6pm ya Thu 6 Nov, 5pm. Dental Cleaning @ ₹299. Reply 1 for the first slot, 2 for the second, or send a time that works better.
rationale: Customer recall message uses name, timing, actual slots, and a low-friction booking CTA.

body: Ramesh, quick heads-up: urgent: atorvastatin batches AT2024-1102, AT2024-1108 from MfrZ are under recall review. You have about 240 chronic-Rx customers — even a small affected subset puts trust at risk if you are not the first to tell them. Pharmacies that communicate recalls proactively retain more trust than those customers hear about it elsewhere. I've already drafted the customer note plus the replacement-pickup workflow. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Supply alert uses loss aversion (trust at risk), social proof (proactive pharmacies retain trust), and effort externalization (already drafted).

body: Hi Mr. Sharma, Apollo Health Plus Pharmacy here. the next refill for metformin, atorvastatin, telmisartan is due soon. We can keep the same medicines ready so there is no gap. Free home delivery can be arranged to your saved address. Reply CONFIRM to dispatch, or tell us if any dosage changed.
rationale: Chronic refill message stays precise, practical, and easy to act on for a caregiver or family contact.

body: Suresh, quick heads-up: good idea. Since your weekday thali is already doing about 18 orders/day, I'd start the corporate version with 10, 25, and 50+ slabs plus one fixed lunch delivery window for Indiranagar offices. I can draft the exact menu and price ladder now. Reply YES?
rationale: Explicit merchant intent is routed straight into action mode instead of more qualifying questions.

body: Hi Padma, nice direction. For a kids yoga summer camp, I'd start with a 4-week format, two age bands, and one parent-trial message before launch. I can draft the timetable and pricing frame in one shot. Reply YES?
rationale: Explicit merchant intent is routed straight into action mode instead of more qualifying questions.

body: Hi Rashmi, PowerHouse Fitness here. It's been about 57 days since your last session. No judgment at all, this happens. We've got a good re-entry point for your earlier weight loss goal, and I can hold one trial session for you. Reply YES if you want me to block it.
rationale: Winback message keeps the tone warm, removes shame, and reduces friction to one simple commitment.

body: Dr. Meera, compliance heads-up: DCI revised radiograph dose limits effective 2026-12-15. Deadline: 15 Dec 2026. Maximum dose per IOPA exposure drops from 1.5 mSv to 1.0 mSv. E-speed film passes at the new limit; D-speed does not. Digital RVG sensors unaffected. Audit your X-ray setup before Dec 15; document E-speed or RVG in your SOPs. Since you were already updating posts recently, I can keep this short for the Lajpat Nagar team. I can turn it into a 3-point clinic checklist you can action in 5 minutes. Reply YES? Dental Council of India circular 2026-11-04
rationale: Compliance-triggered message with deadline, source anchor, and practical next step.

body: Dr. Bharat, your calls are down 50% in the last 7d vs your usual baseline of 12. Current 30-day calls: 4. Peers in metro_solo_practices_2026 average 3.0% CTR vs your 1.8%. Meanwhile 95 of your patients have lapsed in Andheri West. Every week without a recovery move widens that gap. Your GBP is still unverified — that alone costs you trust. You also have no active offer visible right now. I've already identified the single highest-impact fix. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Performance dip uses loss aversion (widening gap), peer social proof, and curiosity (want to see the fix) to drive reply.

body: Dr. Bharat, your Pro plan has 12 days left. Renewal amount is ₹4999. Current 30-day calls are only 4. You do not have an active visible offer live right now. I can draft one renewal summary plus the first recovery move to justify it. Reply YES?
rationale: Renewal prompt keeps the ask transactional and specific instead of slipping back into re-qualification.

body: Hi Kavya, Studio11 Family Salon here. 196 days to the wedding is a good time to start the next prep step. After your trial, the right move now is a structured skin-prep window rather than waiting for the final rush. Haircut @ ₹99. Want me to hold your first consultation slot?
rationale: Bridal follow-up message uses timing, continuity from trial, and a single next-step ask.

body: Hi Karthik (parent: Sumitra), Zen Yoga Studio here. Hope the trial felt useful. The easiest next step is to lock your next session while the rhythm is fresh. I can hold Sat 3 May, 8am for you. Reply YES to confirm it.
rationale: Trial follow-up message leverages recency and offers one concrete slot rather than reopening the whole sales conversation.

body: Suresh, quick heads-up: DC vs MI is at Arun Jaitley Stadium 7:30pm. You already do 180 delivery orders vs 95 dine-in orders in 30 days. Saturday matches usually shift demand toward home orders. Skip a generic match promo and push Buy 1 Pizza Get 1 Free (Tue-Thu) as the sharper angle. I can draft the banner copy now. Reply YES?
rationale: Event-triggered recommendation adds operator judgment and channels the merchant toward an offer that fits match-day behavior.

body: Suresh, quick heads-up: 4 recent reviews mention late delivery, and it's still rising. With 180 delivery orders in the last 30 days, each negative review costs you more than a low-volume place. Most repeated phrasing: "took 50 mins for a 15 min ride". Good news: your food quality is still being praised, so this is an ops fix, not a menu problem. I've already drafted a reply template that addresses this and protects Buy 1 Pizza Get 1 Free (Tue-Thu). Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Review-theme uses loss aversion (costs you more), curiosity (want to see the draft), and effort externalization (already drafted).

body: Vikas, quick heads-up: your Google profile is still unverified, which means you are missing out on trust and discovery compared to verified competitors in Gomti Nagar. They typically see around 30% more traffic. Right now you are getting 720 views but only 14 calls in 30 days, which is a missed opportunity. You also do not have an active visible offer yet, so verification is the cleanest next bottleneck to clear. Path available: postcard or phone call. I can send the 3 exact steps to get verified now, taking just 5 minutes. Reply YES?
rationale: Verification message leverages loss aversion and social proof while maintaining a low-friction 5-minute CTA.

body: Hi Anjali, it's been 38 days since we last spoke — last topic was subscription expiry. Since then, 180 more customers have gone idle in Aundh. Every week without a push widens the gap. Peers in metro_unisex_salons_2026 average 4.0% CTR vs your 2.2%. I've already picked the single best next move and drafted it around Haircut @ ₹99. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Dormancy nudge uses loss aversion (gap widens every week), peer social proof, curiosity (want to see it), and effort externalization (already drafted).

body: Dr. Meera, 3-month fluoride varnish recall outperforms 6-month for high-risk adult caries. For your 124 high-risk adult patients, the strongest hook is this: 2,100-patient data supports the change. Reassess recall interval for adults flagged high-risk in your charting. I can draft one 90-second patient WhatsApp you can use this week. Reply YES? JIDA Oct 2026, p.14
rationale: Research-led message anchored in category digest and merchant-specific cohort, with a low-friction follow-up artifact.

body: Hi Lakshmi, quick check: what service has been most asked for this week at Studio11 Family Salon? If I had to guess, is it Haircut @ ₹99, Hair Spa @ ₹499? You were already seeing bridal-trial search momentum recently. I'll turn your answer into one Google post plus one pricing reply you can reuse this week — ready in 2 minutes. Reply with just the service name.
rationale: Curiosity-led prompt with social proof anchor invites a lightweight reply and offers immediate work output with effort externalization.

body: Hi Anjali, it's been 38 days since your last plan expired, and 24 more customers have gone inactive since then — that number grows every week you wait. Calls are also down about 30%. Your total lapsed pool is already 180. I've already drafted a winback push around Haircut @ ₹99 for your old customers. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Winback uses loss aversion (grows every week), curiosity (want to see it), and effort externalization (already drafted).

body: Ramesh, quick heads-up: summer demand is shifting fast: ORS demand +40, sunscreen demand +38, antifungal demand +45. This is a good shelf and WhatsApp moment for the categories already moving, not the ones cooling off. I can draft a 3-item summer push using your delivery and repeat-customer strengths. Reply YES?
rationale: Seasonal category message converts trend signals into a merchant-facing merchandising action.

body: Dr. Meera, a new competitor just opened 1.3 km away: Smile Studio. They're leading with Dental Cleaning @ ₹199. In Lajpat Nagar, that will pull attention fast unless your hook is sharper. You have 124 high-risk adults in your base already — trust and recall matter more than a price race. I've already drafted comparison-safe positioning copy around Dental Cleaning @ ₹299. Want to see the side-by-side? Reply YES.
rationale: Competitor alert uses loss aversion (pull attention), curiosity (see the side-by-side), and effort externalization (already drafted).

body: Hi Lakshmi, Diwali is 188 days away, so this is planning time, not blast time. For Kapra, the sharper move is to save one festive draft now and keep Haircut @ ₹99 doing the current work. I can draft the Diwali version and park it for later. Reply YES?
rationale: Seasonal message uses event timing plus the merchant's actual offer inventory to create a ready-to-send artifact.

body: Suresh, quick heads-up: you're at 145 reviews and only 5 away from 150. In Indiranagar, crossing that mark is a clean trust signal. Best moment to ask is right after customers redeem Weekday Lunch Thali @ ₹149. I've already drafted a 2-line review ask that feels natural, not needy. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Milestone uses social proof (peer median), loss aversion (puts you ahead), curiosity (see the draft), and effort externalization.

body: Hi Karthik, your views are down 30% this week, but this looks seasonal rather than structural. Apr-Jun is usually a softer acquisition window here. The smart move right now is retention over acquisition — the gyms that grow through summer are the ones that keep their base. You still have 245 active members and a 28% trial-to-paid rate — that is your strongest asset right now. I've already drafted a 7-day attendance challenge to protect member retention. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Seasonal dip uses social proof (smart gyms do X), effort externalization (already drafted), and curiosity (want to see it).

body: Dr. Meera, one CDE item worth your attention: IDA Delhi: Digital impressions — 2026 state of the art. 2 credits, free for members. Especially useful if you are actively discussing aligners or digital workflow. I can pull the 3 takeaways most relevant to your clinic. Reply YES?
rationale: CDE message ties professional development to merchant-facing growth output, which makes the invite more compelling.

body: Hi Padma, your calls are up 15% this week vs a baseline of 18. Likely driver: the kids yoga post. Your trial-to-paid rate is already 55% — compounding this spike now could lock in more paid members before it fades. I've already drafted the next post to double down on First Month @ ₹499 while the momentum is fresh. Want to see it? Reply YES.
rationale: Performance spike uses loss aversion (before it fades), curiosity (want to see the draft), and effort externalization (already drafted).
